strengths: Strong Python and SQL hands-on experience
Proven dashboarding with Power BI and Tableau
Quantified impact on KPIs and operational efficiency
weaknesses: Limited explicit machine learning/predictive model examples
No explicit GLM/cluster/PCA or winsorization mentions
People Analytics / HR domain experience not shown
Unclear formal statistics coursework or depth
Predictive modeling years (3+ in role) not demonstrated
improvement_bullets: Engineered predictive models (linear/logistic regression and cluster analysis) to forecast loan default risk, utilizing feature engineering and validation techniques to improve model accuracy by 15%.
Performed multivariate statistical analysis and non-parametric testing on large-scale datasets to identify high-impact performance trends, informing data-driven strategic planning.
Designed and implemented ETL pipelines that integrate diverse data sources—mimicking HRIS and ATS architectures—to automate reporting on workforce metrics and operational KPIs.
Applied statistical methods including feature creation, validation, and outlier management (winsorization) to refine predictive models, ensuring robust and actionable insights for stakeholders.
Collaborated cross-functionally to translate complex predictive model outputs into executive-level dashboards, directly supporting organizational decision-making and human capital strategy.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Predictive Analytics Analyst opportunity with NYU Langone Health. I am interested in this opportunity because of NYU Langone’s commitment to excellence in patient outcomes and its evidence-based approach to organizational growth. What interests me about working for NYU Langone is its reputation as a premier academic medical center and the scale of its research enterprise, which intrigues me because of the immense potential to apply advanced modeling to complex, mission-driven datasets. I believe my expertise in predictive modeling and my proficiency in Python-based data pipelines would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by blending technical rigor with a deep focus on actionable business outcomes. In fact, while analyzing a 2M+ row loan portfolio, I developed an end-to-end ETL pipeline and built predictive models to segment high-risk borrowers. This enabled stakeholders to visualize risk dimensions in Power BI, directly contributing to more informed, data-backed lending strategies that can be mirrored in workforce planning or turnover analysis. Ultimately, I know that the Predictive Analytics Analyst role at NYU Langone Health would allow me to bring my machine learning skills to optimize HR data utilization. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my cross-functional collaboration skills. I led the automation of intake workflows and translated complex performance data into clear narratives for executive leadership, reducing process bottlenecks by 25%. I am excited by the opportunity to use my statistical modeling and data visualization skills in working on workforce and performance outcomes at NYU Langone Health. I know that my technical background and my ability to simplify complex insights align with what it takes to succeed at the Predictive Analytics Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
